description: An unrelated preset edit recompiles a new generation; a state trigger whose own compiled structure and closed-over values are untouched by that edit carries its first-observation baseline forward and fires normally on a genuine post-swap transition, while a sibling trigger on the same entity whose own for: duration was edited in that same generation has its first-observation baseline reset and does not fire merely by reconfirming the unchanged durable value.

new_generation.note: compiled solely because an unrelated preset (preset_id 01J8Z3K4N5P6Q7R8S9T0V1W2Z5), referenced by a different rule entirely, was edited

rules.note: byte-identical compiled trigger to generation 1; unrelated to the edited preset (RUL-381: unchanged)
for: edited 10->20 in this same generation; compiled structure changed (RUL-381: changed)

events.note: first observation after generation 1 apply; reconfirms durable off; establishes first-observation baseline for both rule-R and rule-S
unrelated preset edit recompiles; rule-R unchanged, rule-S changed
genuine transition, well clear of any stabilization window, occurring after the generation-2 swap

results.reason: rule-R's trigger is unchanged across the generation swap (RUL-381, reused by RUL-303 at the trigger level); its first-observation baseline carries forward from seq 2 (off), so this off->on observation is a genuine transition against the durable last-recorded value, not a reconfirmation — the unrelated preset edit does not reset it

rule-S's trigger changed in generation 2 (for: 10->20), so RUL-303 resets its first-observation baseline on this generation-apply; this seq-4 observation is therefore rule-S's first observation since the reset and MUST NOT be treated as a transition (RUL-300), even though the same off->on change fired rule-R at the same instant — demonstrating first-observation state is kept independently per (trigger, entity), RUL-304, not per entity alone
